```json
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A global logistics platform adding real-time earnings breakdown visibility for drivers across three jurisdictions with materially different labor classifications, legal transparency obligations, and institutional approval requirements. The feature must reconcile a single product concept ('show drivers their pay') with divergent legal realities: mandatory compliance in California, institutional co-determination in Germany, and voluntary disclosure in Indonesia.",
    "bounded_context": "Driver Compensation & Earnings Transparency — intersects with Payroll, Delivery Fulfillment, and Regulatory Compliance contexts",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Base Pay", "definition": "The fixed compensation component for a work period or delivery, calculated differently for employees (hourly/guaranteed minimum) vs independent contractors (per-task rate)" },
      { "term": "Per-Delivery Bonus", "definition": "Variable compensation awarded upon completion of each qualifying delivery, subject to platform-defined bonus rules" },
      { "term": "Tip", "definition": "Voluntary gratuity from the customer passed through to the driver. Legally distinct from wages — must never be counted toward base pay or minimum wage obligations in employee jurisdictions" },
      { "term": "Deduction", "definition": "Any amount subtracted from gross earnings. For employees: legally enumerated (taxes, benefits, authorized withholdings). For contractors: platform fees, equipment charges, or other contractual offsets" },
      { "term": "Earnings Breakdown", "definition": "The itemized real-time view of a driver's compensation components for a given period, showing how gross earnings are derived and what deductions produce net pay" },
      { "term": "Net Pay", "definition": "The final amount payable to the driver after all deductions are applied to gross earnings" },
      { "term": "Itemized Wage Statement", "definition": "California Labor Code §226 requirement: a detailed statement showing gross wages, total hours, piece rates, deductions, net wages, pay period dates, and employer identity" },
      { "term": "Works Council (Betriebsrat)", "definition": "German employee representative body with legal co-determination rights. Must formally approve changes to compensation display systems before deployment under the Works Constitution Act (BetrVG §87)" },
      { "term": "Driver Classification", "definition": "The legal employment status of the driver — employee (California under AB5) or independent contractor (Indonesia) — which determines applicable wage laws, deduction rules, and transparency obligations" }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "Earnings breakdown must be arithmetically consistent: Base Pay + Per-Delivery Bonuses + Tips − Deductions = Net Pay. This equation must hold at all times in every jurisdiction.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "If the displayed breakdown does not sum to the displayed net pay, the system is presenting corrupt financial data. This is a data integrity constraint — no policy can override arithmetic correctness.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any driver in any jurisdiction, the sum of (base_pay + bonuses + tips - deductions) equals the displayed net_pay, with zero tolerance for rounding discrepancy beyond the smallest currency unit."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Tips must never be included in or offset against base pay calculations. Tips are a distinct compensation line item in all jurisdictions.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "In California, using tips to subsidize minimum wage is illegal.       "testable_assertion": "No calculation path exists where a tip amount reduces the base pay or per-delivery bonus amount shown to a driver."
    },

      "rule": "In California, the earnings breakdown must satisfy all itemized wage statement requirements under Labor Code §226: gross wages earned, total hours worked, applicable piece-rate or hourly rate, all deductions (itemized individually), net wages earned, pay period start and end dates, and employer legal name and address.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a legal mandate for employees. Failure to comply exposes the platform to statutory penalties of $50–$4,000 per pay period per employee, plus potential class-action liability. The system is non-compliant — not merely suboptimal — if any required field is missing.",
      "testable_assertion": "For California drivers, the earnings breakdown includes all fields enumerated in Labor Code §226, and no field is null, blank, or omitted."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The earnings breakdown feature must not be deployed in Germany until the applicable Works Council (Betriebsrat) has formally approved the compensation display system change. This is a deployment gate, not a product feature toggle.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Under BetrVG §87(1), the works council has co-determination rights on matters affecting pay transparency and monitoring. Deploying without approval is a legal violation that can result in injunctions and the feature being forcibly disabled. The system state 'deployed in Germany without Betriebsrat approval' is inherently illegal.",
      "testable_assertion": "The feature cannot be activated for any German driver unless a works_council_approval flag is set to true, and that flag can only be set by an authorized compliance action — never by product or engineering."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Each deduction shown to an employee-classified driver (California) must be individually itemized with a description and legal basis. Lump-sum or aggregated deduction lines are not permitted.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "California Labor Code §226 requires individual itemization. A system that aggregates deductions into a single line violates the statute regardless of the total being mathematically correct.",
      "testable_assertion": "For California drivers, every deduction entry has a non-empty description and a deduction_type classification. No single deduction entry aggregates multiple distinct deduction categories."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "For California employee-classified drivers, deductions must not reduce net pay below the applicable minimum wage for hours worked in the pay period.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Paying below minimum wage is illegal for employees under both California and federal law. A system that permits or displays sub-minimum-wage net pay after deductions is encoding a labor law violation.",
      "testable_assertion": "For any California driver pay period, (net_pay / total_hours_worked) >= applicable_minimum_wage_rate."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "In Indonesia, drivers classified as independent contractors must see a service fee or platform commission as a separate line item, not hidden within other deduction categories.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "This is a transparency best practice for contractor relationships, not a legal mandate in Indonesia. However, it reduces disputes and builds trust. A VP could decide to change the display format.",
      "testable_assertion": "For Indonesian contractor drivers, if a platform fee is charged, it appears as a distinct line item labeled as a platform/service fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "Earnings data must reflect completed and confirmed deliveries only. Pending, disputed, or cancelled deliveries must not appear in the earnings breakdown as earned income.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying unconfirmed earnings as actual pay corrupts the financial record and could constitute misrepresentation. A driver making financial decisions based on unearned displayed income creates liability for the platform.",
      "testable_assertion": "Every line item in the earnings breakdown references a delivery with status = 'completed' or 'confirmed'. No delivery with status 'pending', 'disputed', or 'cancelled' contributes to displayed earnings."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Currency and locale formatting must match the driver's operating jurisdiction. California displays in USD, Germany in EUR, Indonesia in IDR.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Displaying earnings in the wrong currency is not a formatting preference — it is materially incorrect financial information that could lead to tax reporting errors and driver confusion.",
      "testable_assertion": "A California driver sees amounts in USD, a German driver in EUR, and an Indonesian driver in IDR, with locale-appropriate formatting (decimal separators, currency symbols)."
    }

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A California employee driver has completed 3 deliveries in the current pay period",

    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A driver in any jurisdiction views their earnings breakdown",
      "when": "They manually sum all displayed components (base + bonuses + tips − deductions)",
      "then": "The result exactly matches the displayed net pay amount"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "A driver completes a delivery but the customer's tip is still processing (tip finalization delay)", "expected_behavior": "The delivery earnings appear without the tip.     { "scenario": "A California driver works a shift with zero deliveries (idle/on-call time)", "expected_behavior": "Base pay for hours worked must still appear in the breakdown.
```
